content: I'm Jordan, 29, based in Singapore. Backend engineer at Northwind Pay - a mid-size fintech - for about three and a half years now.
Our backend team is six engineers plus two SREs and one EM. I'm one of two focusing on the payments core; the others own onboarding, reconciliation, and reporting.
Most of my work is in Go 1.22, with Postgres 15 as the source of truth and Redis for hot caches. gRPC between services, Kafka for the async stuff.
Before Northwind I spent two years at a logistics startup doing PHP, then switched to Go and never looked back. The concurrency model just clicks for me.
I work from home most days from my flat on the east side of Singapore. The office is in the CBD but I only go in for planning and postmortems.
Finished the auth refactor on acme-api today - split the monolithic middleware into per-route scopes. The PR is up, just waiting on Priya's review.
dejaview-demo is shaping up: the timeline ingest pipeline is wired up, and I got the embeddings flowing into pgvector. Next step is the novelty gate so we stop storing duplicate frames.

p95 on the checkout endpoint of acme-api is stuck at 340ms. The plan is idempotent retry with exponential backoff plus moving the fraud check off the hot path. Target is under 200ms by end of quarter.
Migrations for the new idempotency-key table are written. I reviewed them table-by-table like always - the last one backfills 12M rows so I'm batching it to avoid locking writers.
Kafka consumer lag on the reconciliation topic spiked again last night. I think it's the new batch flush logic; going to add per-partition metrics before I touch anything.
Cut a new build of dejaview-demo with the Honcho deriver hooked up. Atomic facts are landing in the user model - first time I can actually ask it what I was doing last Tuesday and get a sane answer.
Neovim with a fairly minimal config - lazy.nvim, telescope, lspconfig, and that's about it. I tried VS Code for a month but the modal editing hole was too big.
tmux all day. One window per project, three panes - editor, tests, repl. Never could get into the IDE-embedded-terminal workflow.
Devcontainer on an Ubuntu 22.04 base image. Means the new hires get a working toolchain in five minutes instead of two days of works-on-my-machine.
Wireshark over tcpdump for gRPC debugging every time. Being able to dissect the HTTP/2 frames and see the actual message bodies beats squinting at hex.
Self-hosted over SaaS where it makes sense - I run my own Gitea on a home NAS for personal projects, and I'd always rather own the Postgres and Redis config than fight a vendor's defaults.
I do my deep work 7 to 11 in the morning, before standup. Afternoons are for reviews, pairing, and the slack-and-meetings tax. Anything that needs real focus gets scheduled into the AM block.
Database migrations get a table-by-table review before I apply them - schema, indexes, the backfill plan, the rollback. I've seen one too many lock-the-whole-table incidents to wing it.
Tests before the PR, no exceptions. Go's table-driven tests plus an integration suite on testcontainers. If CI is green and coverage on the touched paths is over 80 percent, I'm comfortable shipping.
Async over sync for anything that can wait. I'd rather write a one-paragraph doc comment than schedule a meeting - most quick calls are just badly-written documents.
I keep a running decisions log in the repo - just a markdown file with dated entries. Saves the same architecture argument every three months when someone new joins.
Hit ECONNRESET storms during the nightly batch uploads again. The retries amplify it - looks like the upstream load balancer is dropping idle connections at exactly 60 seconds. Tuning keepalive next.

Postgres threw PG-08006 connection failures on the reporting pod this morning. Pool exhaustion - peak of 90 concurrent out of a max 100. Bumped the pool and added pgbouncer in transaction mode as a stopgap.
acme-api's been logging GRPC-14 unavailable intermittently. Traced it to the fraud service timing out at exactly 2.5 seconds - its own DB is the bottleneck. Filed a ticket with the fraud team.
Memory on the dejaview-demo embedding worker keeps climbing - looks like a leak in the HNSW index build. pprof points at the allocator, going to cap the batch size and force a GC between batches.

Wireshark showed the gRPC stream on reconciliation is sending pings every 30s but the server ACKs late, so the client hits keepalive-timeout and tears down. Flipped the policy to allow more in-flight pings.

Anyone got a rule of thumb for when to split a Kafka topic vs. add partitions? Our reconciliation topic is at 24 partitions and consumer lag is still rough - wondering if I'm patching the wrong layer.
What's the current best practice for idempotency keys on a payments API - server-generated UUID vs. client-supplied hash of the request? I've seen both, the pros and cons aren't obvious to me.
For pgvector HNSW indexes - m=16 ef_construction=64 has been fine for 1M vectors, but I'm projecting 50M. Anyone scaled past that on a single node, or is it time to shard?
How do you all keep deriver prompts from drifting? Mine extract great for a week, then edge cases creep in and I'm patching few-shots again. Curious if there's a regression-test pattern that works.
